searchDocs.title: Installation​
searchDocs.content: Add Cashier to your wally.toml:  [dependencies] Cashier = "yuzkkj/monetization-service@1.4.0"   Then run:  wally install

searchDocs.title: Setting Up​
searchDocs.content: Cashier has a server and a client module. Both need to be initialized before anything else.

searchDocs.title: Server​
searchDocs.content: local DataService = require(path.to.DataService).server local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server DataService:init() -- DataService must be initialized first Cashier:init({ data = { { name = "VIP", type = "Gamepass", id = 123456, giftId = 654321, callback = function(player: Player) DataService:arrayInsert(player, "titles", "VIP") return true end, }, { name = "500 Coins", type = "DevProduct", id = 789012, callback = function(player: Player) DataService:update(player, "coins", function(coins) return coins + 500 end) return true end, }, }, limitedsCallbacks = { ["Exclusive Sword"] = function(player: Player) DataService:arrayInsert(player, "weapons", "Exclusive Sword") return true end, }, })

searchDocs.title: Client​
searchDocs.content: local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client Cashier:init()   That's it. The client fetches item data from the server automatically.

searchDocs.title: Purchasing Items​
searchDocs.content: On the client, call purchaseItem with the item name. Cashier handles the prompt, receipt processing, and callback execution.  local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local buyButton = script.Parent buyButton.Activated:Connect(function() Cashier:purchaseItem("VIP") end)   You can listen for when a purchase finishes:  Cashier.purchaseFinished:Connect(function(itemName, wasSuccessful, wasGift) if wasSuccessful then print("Bought", itemName) end end)

searchDocs.title: Predicates and Extra Params​
searchDocs.content: Some purchases need validation or extra context. Use predicate to gate the purchase and pass extra arguments through:  -- Server config { name = "Upgrade Unit", type = "DevProduct", id = 111222, predicate = function(player: Player, unitId: string) -- Validate that the unit exists and isn't maxed return UnitService:canUpgrade(player, unitId) end, callback = function(player: Player, unitId: string) UnitService:upgrade(player, unitId) return true end, }   -- Client Cashier:purchaseItem("Upgrade Unit", selectedUnit.id)   The extra params are passed to both the predicate and the callback.

searchDocs.title: Gifting​
searchDocs.content: Players can gift items to each other. The recipient gets the item even if they're offline — it'll be delivered next time they join.  -- Client local success, errorMessage = Cashier:gift(recipientUserId, "VIP") if not success then warn(errorMessage) end   You can override onGiftReceived on the client to show a notification:  function Cashier:onGiftReceived(giftData) print(giftData.from, "gifted you", giftData.item) end

searchDocs.title: Checking Ownership​
searchDocs.content: -- Client if Cashier:hasItem("VIP") then -- Show VIP perks end -- Server if Cashier:hasItem(player, "VIP") then -- Grant VIP perks end

searchDocs.title: Limited Items​
searchDocs.content: Limiteds are stock-capped items that can expire. They're stored in a DataStore and synced to all clients in real time.

searchDocs.title: Adding a Limited​
searchDocs.content: -- Server Cashier:addLimited({ name = "Exclusive Sword", id = 333444, -- Developer product id initialStock = 100, stockLeft = 100, onePerPlayer = true, expiration = os.time() + 86400, -- Available for 24 hours })

searchDocs.title: Purchasing a Limited​
searchDocs.content: -- Client Cashier:purchaseLimited("Exclusive Sword")   The client checks stock and expiration locally before even asking the server. The server re-validates everything before prompting.

searchDocs.title: Reacting to Changes​
searchDocs.content: The limitedChanged signal fires whenever a limited's stock or expiration updates:  -- Client Cashier.limitedChanged:Connect(function(limited) print(limited.name, "now has", limited.stockLeft, "left") end)

searchDocs.title: Managing Stock and Time​
searchDocs.content: -- Server Cashier:addStockToLimited("Exclusive Sword", 50) Cashier:addTimeToLimited("Exclusive Sword", 3600) -- +1 hour Cashier:removeLimited("Exclusive Sword")

searchDocs.title: Item Types​
searchDocs.content: Type	Behavior"Gamepass"	Purchased once, ownership persists across sessions via Roblox's gamepass system "Semipass"	Purchased once, ownership tracked in DataService (not a real gamepass) "DevProduct"	Can be purchased multiple times, not tracked as "owned"

searchDocs.title: Notes​
searchDocs.content: Always initialize DataService before Cashier on the server.The callback should return true if the item was successfully granted. Returning false will cause the receipt to be retried later.Limiteds use an oversell strategy: once a player is in the purchase flow, the item is always delivered even if stock hits zero from another purchase. Stock acts as a soft cap for new prompts.

searchDocs.title: Next Steps​
searchDocs.content: Check the full API reference for every method, signal, and type available:  CashierServer — server-side item registration, purchase processing, gifting, and limiteds management.CashierClient — client-side purchase prompts, item queries, and real-time limited updates.

searchDocs.title: ItemType​
searchDocs.content: </> type ItemType = "DevProduct" | "Gamepass" | "Semipass" Determines the type of an item.

searchDocs.title: Item​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Item { name: string-- The name of the item description: string-- The description of the item iconId: string-- The icon of the item. This is a full "rbxassetid://..." string type: ItemType-- The type of the item id: number-- The id of the gamepass of developer product linked with the item giftId: number?-- The id of the developer product for gifting this item } Useful information of an item.

searchDocs.title: Limited​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Limited { name: string-- The name of the limited id: number-- The developer product id linked to the limited initialStock: number-- The initial stock when the limited was created stockLeft: number-- The remaining stock available for purchase onePerPlayer: boolean-- Determines if a player can only purchase this limited once expiration: number?-- The Unix timestamp at which the limited expires } Configuration and state data for a stock-limited item.

searchDocs.title: GiftData​
searchDocs.content: </> interface GiftData { item: string-- The name of the item from: number-- The id of the user that has gifted the item } Data from a gift.

searchDocs.title: purchaseStarted​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient.purchaseStarted: Signal<> A signal that fires when a purchase prompt is shown to the player.

searchDocs.title: purchaseFinished​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient.purchaseFinished: Signal<string,boolean,boolean> A signal that fires when a purchase prompt is closed, providing the name of the item, a boolean indicating whether the purchase was completed successfully, and a boolean indicating if the purchase was a gift.

searchDocs.title: limitedChanged​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient.limitedChanged: Signal<Limited> A signal that fires whenever a limited's data changes, providing the updated limited info.

searchDocs.title: init​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:init() → () Initializes the client-side of Cashier. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client Cashier:init() WARNING :init(...) should be called before using any other method.

searchDocs.title: getAllItems​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:getAllItems() → {Item} Returns an array with all registered items. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client for _, item in Cashier:getAllItems() do print(item.name, item.type) end

searchDocs.title: getItemById​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:getItemById(itemId: number) → Item? Retrieves the info of an item with the given id.itemId can be either a normal id or a giftId. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local item = Cashier:getItemById(1234) for k, v in item do print(k, v) end

searchDocs.title: getItemByName​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:getItemByName(itemName: string) → Item? Retrieves the info of an item with the given name. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local item = Cashier:getItemByName("VIP") for k, v in item do print(k, v) end

searchDocs.title: getItemsOfType​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:getItemsOfType(itemType: ItemType) → {Item} Returns an array with all items of the given type. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client for _, item in Cashier:getItemsOfType("Gamepass") do print(item.name, item.id) end

searchDocs.title: isGiftId​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:isGiftId(id: number) → boolean Checks if an id is the giftId of an item. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local isGiftId = Cashier:isGiftId(4321) print(isGiftId)

searchDocs.title: getItemPrice​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> CashierClient:getItemPrice(itemName: string) → number Returns the price in robux of an item. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local priceLabel = script.Parent local price = Cashier:getItemPrice("VIP") priceLabel.Text = `${price}`

searchDocs.title: hasItem​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:hasItem(itemName: string) → boolean Checks if the user has the item with the given name. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local textLabel = script.Parent local hasItem = Cashier:hasItem("VIP") if hasItem then textLabel.Text = "Owned" end

searchDocs.title: purchaseItem​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:purchaseItem( itemName: string, ...: any?-- Extra params passed to the item's predicate ) → () Prompts the local player to purchase the item with the given name. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local button = script.Parent button.Activated:Connect(function() Cashier:purchaseItem("VIP") end)

searchDocs.title: gift​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:gift( userId: number, itemName: string ) → ( boolean,-- Whether the gift was successfully initiated string?-- An error message in case the user could not gift the other one ) Prompts the user to purchase the item for the other player. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local giftButton = script.Parent giftButton.Activated:Connect(function() Cashier:gift(6969, "VIP") end)

searchDocs.title: onGiftReceived​
searchDocs.content: Override </> CashierClient:onGiftReceived(giftData: GiftData) → () Overridable method called when the user receives a gift. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client function Cashier:onGiftReceived(giftData: GiftData) print(`{giftData.from} gifted you {giftData.item}!`) end

searchDocs.title: getAllLimiteds​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:getAllLimiteds() → {Limited} Returns an array with all currently available limited items. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client for _, limited in Cashier:getAllLimiteds() do print(limited.name, limited.stockLeft) end

searchDocs.title: getLimitedByName​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:getLimitedByName(name: string) → Limited? Retrieves a limited item by its name. Returns nil if no limited with the given name exists. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local limited = Cashier:getLimitedByName("Exclusive Sword") if limited then print(limited.stockLeft) end

searchDocs.title: getLimitedFromId​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:getLimitedFromId(id: number) → Limited? Retrieves a limited item by its developer product id. Returns nil if no limited with the given id exists. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local limited = Cashier:getLimitedFromId(123456) if limited then print(limited.name, limited.stockLeft) end

searchDocs.title: purchaseLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:purchaseLimited(limitedName: string) → () Prompts the local player to purchase a limited item. Performs client-side validation (stock, expiration, one-per-player) before requesting the server. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client local button = script.Parent button.Activated:Connect(function() Cashier:purchaseLimited("Exclusive Sword") end)

searchDocs.title: hasLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierClient:hasLimited(limitedName: string) → boolean Checks if the local player owns the given limited item. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client if Cashier:hasLimited("Exclusive Sword") then print("Player owns the Exclusive Sword!") end

searchDocs.title: ItemType​
searchDocs.content: </> type ItemType = "DevProduct" | "Gamepass" | "Semipass" Determines the type of an item.

searchDocs.title: ItemData​
searchDocs.content: </> interface ItemData { name: string-- The name of the item type: ItemType-- The type of the item id: number-- The id of the gamepass or developer product linked with the item giftId: number?-- The id of the developer product for gifting this item callback: (( Player , ...any? ) → boolean)?-- A callback to be called when delivering the item to a player predicate: (( player: Player , ...any ) → boolean)?-- A function to check if the user can purchase the item with the given params } Configuration data for an item registered with Cashier. local items = { { name = "VIP", type = "Gamepass", id = 1234, giftId = 4321, callback = function(player: Player) DataService:arrayInsert(player, "titles", "VIP") -- grants any extra benefits here return true end }, { name = "Starter Pack", type = "Semipass", id = 7890, giftId = 987, callback = function(player: Player) DataService:update(player, "cash", function(value) return value + 5000 end) return true end }, { name = "Upgrade Unit", type "DevProduct", id = 6969, predicate = function(player: Player, unitId: string) -- make any validation here, for example -- if unitId is not nill -- if it is a valid unitId -- if it's not at max level, etc. return true end, callback = function(player: Player, unitId: string) -- the unitId will be the same one given to the predicate return true end }, } WARNING If the type of the item is set to "Gamepass", the id should also be a id of a gamepass.

searchDocs.title: Item​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Item { name: string type: ItemType id: number-- The id of the gamepass of developer product linked with the item giftId: number?-- The id of the developer product for gifting this item } Useful information of an item.

searchDocs.title: GiftData​
searchDocs.content: </> interface GiftData { item: string-- The name of the item from: number-- The id of the user that has gifted the item } Data from a gift.

searchDocs.title: PurchaseData​
searchDocs.content: </> interface PurchaseData { player: Player -- The player who bought the item item: string-- The name of the item robuxSpent: number-- The amout of robux spent on the purchase recipientId: number?-- The id of the recipient if the purchase was an gift } Data from a purchase.

searchDocs.title: Limited​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Limited { name: string-- The name of the limited id: number-- The developer product id linked to the limited initialStock: number-- The initial stock when the limited was created stockLeft: number-- The remaining stock available for purchase onePerPlayer: boolean-- Determines if a player can only purchase this limited once expiration: number?-- The Unix timestamp at which the limited expires } Configuration and state data for a stock-limited item.

searchDocs.title: InitOptions​
searchDocs.content: </> type InitOptions = {ItemData} Options for initializing the Cashier.

searchDocs.title: purchaseCompleted​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer.purchaseCompleted: Signal<PurchaseData> A signal that fires when a player buys an item, providing the PurchaseData.

searchDocs.title: init​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:init(options: InitOptions) → () Initializes the server-side of Cashier. local DataService = require(path.to.DataService).server local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local items = require(path.to.items) local limitedsCallback = require(path.to.limitedsCallback) Cashier:init({ itemsData = items, limitedsCallback = limitedsCallback }) WARNING :init(...) should be called before using any other method.

searchDocs.title: getAllItems​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getAllItems() → {Item} Returns the Item of all items. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server for _, item in Cashier:getAllItems() do print(item) end

searchDocs.title: getItemById​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getItemById(itemId: number) → Item? Retrieves the info of an item with the given id.itemId can be either a normal id or a giftId. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local item = Cashier:getItemById(1234) for k, v in item do print(k, v) end

searchDocs.title: getItemByName​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getItemByName(itemName: string) → Item? Retrieves the info of an item with the given name. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local item = Cashier:getItemByName("VIP") for k, v in item do print(k, v) end

searchDocs.title: getItemsOfType​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getItemsOfType(itemType: ItemType) → () Returns an array with all items of the given type. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server for _, item in Cashier:getItemsOfType("Gamepass") do print(item) end

searchDocs.title: isGiftId​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:isGiftId(id: number) → boolean Checks if an id is the giftId of an item. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local isGiftId = Cashier:isGiftId(4321) print(isGiftId)

searchDocs.title: getItemPrice​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getItemPrice(itemName: string) → number Returns the price in robux of an item.

searchDocs.title: hasItem​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:hasItem( player: Player , itemName: string ) → boolean Checks if the user has the item with the given name. local Players = game:GetService("Players") local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local player = Players:GetPlayers()[1] if player then local hasItem = Cashier:hasItem(player, "VIP") print(hasItem) end

searchDocs.title: giveItem​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:giveItem( player: Player , itemName: string ) → boolean Gives the item to the player and calls the callback of the ItemData, if it exists. local Players = game:GetService("Players") local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server Cashier:giveItem(Players.IYuzkkj, "VIP")

searchDocs.title: addItemToInventory​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:addItemToInventory( player: Player , itemName: string, amount: number? ) → () Adds the item to the player inventory. local Players = game:GetService("Players") local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server Cashier:addItemToInventory(Players.IYuzkkj, "VIP") NOTE Further gift attempts of the same item will be sent without charging the user.

searchDocs.title: addGift​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:addGift( userId: number, giftData: GiftData ) → boolean-- Tells if the gift was successfully added Adds the item to the player's pending gifts. The player will receive the item if it is online in another server, or the next time they join the game. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server Cashier:addGift(6969, { item = "VIP", from = 9696 })

searchDocs.title: purchaseItem​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:purchaseItem( player: Player , itemName: string, ...: any-- Extra params ) → () Prompts the user to purchase the item with the given name. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).client if not hasVIP then Cashier:purchaseItem(player, "VIP") end

searchDocs.title: gift​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:gift( player: Player , userId: number, itemName: string ) → ( boolean,-- Tells if the gift was successfully initiated string?-- An error message in case the user could not gift the other one ) Prompts the user to purchase the item for the other player.

searchDocs.title: getAllLimiteds​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getAllLimiteds() → {Limited} Returns an array with all currently available limited items. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server for _, limited in Cashier:getAllLimiteds() do print(limited.name, limited.stockLeft) end

searchDocs.title: getLimitedByName​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getLimitedByName(name: string) → Limited? Retrieves a limited item by its name. Returns nil if no limited with the given name exists. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local limited = Cashier:getLimitedByName("Exclusive Sword") if limited then print(limited.stockLeft) end

searchDocs.title: getLimitedById​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:getLimitedById(id: number) → Limited? Retrieves a limited item by its developer product id. Returns nil if no limited with the given id exists. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local limited = Cashier:getLimitedById(123456) if limited then print(limited.name, limited.stockLeft) end

searchDocs.title: addLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:addLimited(limited: Limited) → () Registers a new limited item. Writes it to the DataStore and notifies all connected clients. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server Cashier:addLimited({ name = "Exclusive Sword", id = 123456, initialStock = 100, stockLeft = 100, onePerPlayer = true, expiration = os.time() + 86400, })

searchDocs.title: addStockToLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:addStockToLimited( limitedName: string, amount: number ) → () Adds stock to an existing limited item. Increments both stockLeft and initialStock. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server Cashier:addStockToLimited("Exclusive Sword", 50)

searchDocs.title: addTimeToLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:addTimeToLimited( limitedName: string, amount: number ) → () Extends the expiration of a limited item by amount seconds. If the limited has no expiration set, it starts from the current time. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server -- Extend by 1 hour Cashier:addTimeToLimited("Exclusive Sword", 3600)

searchDocs.title: removeLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:removeLimited(limitedName: string) → () Removes a limited item from the DataStore and notifies all connected clients of the removal. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server Cashier:removeLimited("Exclusive Sword")

searchDocs.title: purchaseLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:purchaseLimited( player: Player , limitedName: string ) → () Validates and prompts the player to purchase a limited item. Checks that the limited exists, has stock remaining, is not expired, and respects the one-per-player constraint. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server local success = Cashier:purchaseLimited(player, "Exclusive Sword") if not success then warn("Could not prompt limited purchase") end

searchDocs.title: hasLimited​
searchDocs.content: </> CashierServer:hasLimited( player: Player , limitedName: string ) → boolean Checks if the player owns the given limited item. local Cashier = require(path.to.Cashier).server if Cashier:hasLimited(player, "Exclusive Sword") then print("Player already owns this limited!") end
